meta_title: For Freelancers: design with AI to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to design with AI and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Design with AI to Solve Debugging Issues
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with broken code. Learn how to design with AI, leverage Antigravity and Claude Code to debug faster, and ship professional apps for clients.
content.answer_block: Stop chasing syntax errors manually. By designing your architecture with AI, you shift from reactive debugging to proactive construction. Use Antigravity to visualize logic flows and Claude Code to automate refactoring, turning hours of troubleshooting into minutes of precision-engineered, production-ready code that keeps your freelance projects on schedule.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking, your client is waiting, and your terminal is screaming a wall of red text that makes no sense. We have all been there: trapped in a recursive loop of 'fixing' one bug only to spawn three more. It is the silent killer of freelance momentum and profit margins.

But what if you stopped acting like a janitor for your own code? By shifting your workflow to design-first AI development, you stop treating symptoms and start architecting solutions. This is the blueprint for reclaiming your time and delivering high-impact, bug-free applications with surgical precision.

steps.title: Map the Architecture with Antigravity
steps.description: Before touching code, visualize your data flow. This prevents logical bugs before they are even written.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a high-level component architecture diagram for a [Project Type] app, focusing on state management and API integration points.

steps.title: Isolate the Bug Scope
steps.description: Use Claude Code to identify the specific file and function chains causing the runtime failure.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a diagnostic report for the [Error Message] appearing in the [Module Name] module.

steps.title: Design the Fix Pattern
steps.description: Instead of patching, design the refactor to align with the original architecture.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Propose a robust refactoring strategy for the identified bug that adheres to DRY principles.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --plan 'Refactor the data fetching logic to handle null states gracefully'

steps.title: Execute Atomic Refactoring
steps.description: Apply the fix using Claude Code to ensure no side effects are introduced to dependent modules.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the implementation code for the refactor designed in step 3, ensuring backward compatibility.

steps.title: Automated Verification
steps.description: Run a final sweep to ensure the fix is permanent and the system remains stable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a suite of unit tests that cover the bug scenario to prevent future regressions.

pro_tips: Always maintain a 'context.md' file in your root; Claude Code uses this to understand your project's history and constraints.
Use Antigravity to generate pseudo-code before implementation to verify logic correctness without the overhead of debugging syntax.
When a bug is complex, ask Claude to 'explain the state transition' to find where the data is actually failing.
Create a custom library of 'Antigravity Patterns' for common client requests to reduce your setup time by 80%.
Treat your terminal as a command center; keep Claude Code running in a split pane to continuously monitor for linting errors.
content.next_steps: After completing this workflow, begin building your own 'Component Library' using these same AI-driven steps to standardize your freelance output.
content.conversion_block: Stop settling for slow development and constant rework. Master the AI-first workflow today and double your output while cutting your stress in half. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
